Notice Of And Motion To Compel Responses To Plaintiffs Request For Production Of Documents And Motion For Protective Order To Stay Plaintiffs Deposition
Off Calendar Jul-22-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. JASON ERLICH, ESQ. of McCormack & Erlich, Tel No. 415-296-8420 appearing for the Plaintiff;
Atty. WILLIAM C. TEELING, ESQ. of Vartain Law Group, Tel No. 415-391-1155 appearing for Defendant Paymentwall, Inc.

RULING - Both sides stipulate to matter being heard by the pro tem judge. Matter argued and submitted by counsel. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Plaintiff's motion to compel responses to Plaintiff's Request For Production Of Documents is denied as moot. Defendants served a set of responses on June 19, 2015, the same day that Plaintiff appears to have initially served her motion to compel. The responses on their face appear not to be in compliance with the Code of Civil Procedure or drawn in good faith but that specific issue is not before the Court on the current motion (the current motion is brought under CCP section 2031.300 (failure to serve responses); a separate meet and confer process and a new motion under section 2031.310 are required to compel further responses). Plaintiff's motion for a protective order is denied. Under the Code of Civil Procedure there is no priority or staging of discovery other than a statutory right of defendants to serve a notice of deposition in the first 20 days after the defendant has been served with the complaint. Thus, Defendants' efforts to depose Plaintiff and Plaintiff's efforts to obtain documents from Defendants may proceed simultaneously and Plaintiff has no statutory right to require compliance with her document requests before submitting to deposition. The Court further notes that the first notice of deposition was served by Defendants on April 21, 2015 and Plaintiff's requests for production of documents were first served on April 24, 2015. Plaintiff's request for sanctions is denied. This denial is without prejudice to a request for sanctions in connection with any motion under section 2031.310 to compel further responses. Order signed. Judge Pro Tem: Sanford Kingsley, Clerk: Sean Kane, Not Reported. (302/JPT)
